communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the California Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

I need a toxicology report for Gustavo Billanueva Vargas, DOB 01/16/1961. He was killed in an automobile accident on 01/11/2026 in Fresno Ca on HWY 99S.

I am attempting to file a claim for his accidental death life insurance policy. I need the toxicology report for this claim.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 calendar days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Myra Askew

title: Service Partnership Agreement – CBSO and School of Music and Dance/Oregon Bach Festival

communications.subject: Oregon Public Records Law Request: Service Partnership Agreement – CBSO and School of Music and Dance/Oregon Bach Festival
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Oregon Public Records Law, I hereby request the following records:

1. Smartsheet Cell History / Row History – September 3, 2025 Layoff Justification Form

The full Cell History and Row History, including all comments and changes, associated with the Smartsheet submission of the Layoff Justification Form submitted on September 3, 2025 at approximately 3:59 PM and received by ELR at approximately 4:01 PM relating to the elimination of the Accounting Technician position and the September 8, 2025 Layoff Notice issued to Kelvin Shivers II (which may appear in the form as “Kelvin Shivers” without the Roman numeral).

This request is limited to that specific Smartsheet row entry and includes all recorded edits, change history, user attribution, timestamps, and comments associated with that submission.
 
2. Service Partnership Agreement – CBSO and School of Music and Dance/Oregon Bach Festival

A copy of the final, executed Service Partnership Agreement (SPA) or other formal written agreement governing services between CBSO and the School of Music and Dance and/or the Oregon Bach Festival that was in effect at any time between August 1, 2025 and December 31, 2025.

If no executed agreement was in effect between CBSO and the School of Music and Dance and/or the Oregon Bach Festival during that period, please confirm that in writing.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days.

Sincerely,

Kelvin Shivers II

title: $220M DHS Ad Campaign Approval (Department of Homeland Security)

communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

1. Any record reflecting the approval of a reported $220 million DHS ad campaign testified about by Secretary Noem during the March 3, 2026, Senate Judiciary Committee Hearing in response to Sen. Kennedy and Sen. Schiff. 

2. All metrics in existence as of March 3, 2026, reflecting the total reach (in views, individuals, or impressions) of the above-referenced campaign. 

3. Any records reflecting the success of the contract as compared to its deliverables. 

I respectfully seek a fee waiver and expedited processing of this request, and in support of that request, attest under penalty of perjury pursuant to 28 USC 1746 that the following statements are true and correct to the best of my knowledge and understanding. 

1. I am a member of the media primarily engaged in information dissemination. www.MuckRack.com/Andrew-Free. I am a credentialed journalist with the Atlanta Community Press Collective, and a freelancer for national media outlets. I am a member of Investigative Reporters and Editors, which is open only to journalists, and a 2025 grant recipient of the Fund for Investigative Journalism. 

2. There is an urgency need to inform the public about the truth of Secretary Noem's statements to the Senate Judiciary Committee today. Specifically, she claimed under oath that she did not approve or have any role in approving the nearly quarter-billion dollar DHS media campaign referenced during today's hearing. 

3. The campaign is prominently featured on DHS government website and social media posts, which were last updated AFTER the government shutdown: (e.g., https://www.dhs.gov/news/2025/02/17/dhs-announces-ad-campaign-warning-illegal-aliens-self-deport-and-stay-out).

4. The contract and subsequent performance under it has been the subject of widespread media coverage about which there exist questions as to the government's conduct. See, e.g.,
a. https://www.govexec.com/oversight/2025/11/lawmakers-call-probe-how-firm-tied-kristi-noem-got-piece-220m-dhs-ad-contracts/409786/
b. https://www.propublica.org/article/kristi-noem-dhs-ad-campaign-strategy-group
c. http://wgntv.com/hill-politics/noem-faces-gop-heat-over-220m-ad-that-boosted-your-name-recognition/
d. https://www.rollingstone.com/politics/politics-features/trump-kristi-noem-200-million-dhs-ad-campaign-thanks-president-1235276324/
e. https://www.propublica.org/article/kristi-noem-dhs-strategy-group-senate-probe
f. https://www.yahoo.com/news/articles/dhs-cited-border-national-emergency-201743631.html
g. https://www.capjournal.com/news/firm-tied-to-kristi-noem-secretly-got-money-from-220-million-dhs-ad-contracts/article_51843a1a-2f97-4508-a5c1-3d31221d638d.html

5. Lawmakers in Congress are presently at an impasse over DHS's funding, prompting a lapse in annual appropriations and a partial shutdown of certain DHS functions. 

6. There is an urgency to obtain government records regarding these records in a timely fashion so as to make available records to decisionmakers prior to the passage of another DHS funding bill.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Andrew Free

title: Marines support for TV documentary series "Behind the Wings" (2019-present)

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: Marines support for TV documentary series "Behind the Wings" (2019-present)
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

All documents pertaining to collaboration between the US Marine Corps and the production of the TV documentary series "Behind the Wings" (2019-present). According to a document released to me by the Marines through FOIA (see link below), the Marines provided support to this production. I therefore request any and all documents pertaining to Marine Corps support for "Behind the Wings." Such documents may include but should not be not limited to: script notes; production assistance agreements; photographs; and all records of communications between employees of the Marines and members of the cast, crew, and production company of "Behind the Wings," including memos, notes of meetings and conference calls, emails, and other forms of communication.

Here is a link to the aforementioned document:

https://app.box.com/s/t7tbqia68nc136qisb88aw4yvsoajzem

I am the Director of Research Operations at a 501(c)(3) non-profit organization called The Decoding Culture Foundation as well as the editor-in-chief and a contributor to a research magazine published by said foundation titled "Cultural Engineering Studies." These documents will be used for public education and research purposes. I therefore request a waiver of all fees that may otherwise apply. Attached is a document with organization letterhead confirming my position, as well as the organization's 501(c)(3) application and approval form

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Brett Carollo

communications.subject: DC Freedom of Information Act Request: DC Verra Request
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the DC Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

All contracts related to automated enforcement (originals and extensions/amendments) with Verra Mobility (also known as American Traffic Solutions) for DC's school bus stop arm camera program and automated speed enforcement program.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 15 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Jon Smith
